title: Deathstick
content:

{{Youmay|the [[assassin]]|[[death stick]]s, the illicit substance}}

{{Quote|Deathstick kills because she feels completely, utterly alone—and on some level, she wants other people to feel that way too.|Lady Qi'ra, on Deathstick|<ref name="Crimson Reign 2">[[Crimson Reign 2|''Crimson Reign'' 2]]</ref>}}
'''Deathstick''' was a [[Gender|female]] [[Dathomirian]] [[assassin]] and daughter of the [[Nightsisters|Nightsister]] [[Shelish]], active during the [[Imperial Era|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire]]. She originally started out as a street criminal, but once she became known for her skills as an assassin for the [[Crimson Dawn]] and [[Kouhun (order)|Kouhun]], few later sought an audience with her.

Deathstick was a contributor to the rise of Crimson Dawn [[Standard year|years]] after the [[death]] of its leader, former [[Sith Lord]] [[Maul]], under the instruction of new [[Lady]] [[Qi'ra]]. Deathstick and her Kouhun joined the [[Uprising]] and helped fight the Empire in the [[Anoat sector]] of [[the galaxy]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
[[File:DeathstickPast-CrimsonReign2.png|thumb|left|250px|After the Battle of Dathomir, the Nightsisters were all but extinct, and Deathstick was left with the knowledge that she was once part of something greater than herself.]]
The mysterious [[Gender|woman]] known as Deathstick was the daughter of [[Shelish]], a member of the [[Nightsisters|Nightsister]] [[Dathomiri clan|coven]] of [[Dathomirian|Dathomiri]] [[witch]]es<ref name="SWU" /> [[Homeworld|native]] to the<ref name="Fallen Order">''[[Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order]]''</ref> [[Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="TFAVD">''[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> [[planet]] [[Dathomir]].<ref name="Fallen Order" /> In [[20 BBY]],<ref name="Timelines">''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]''</ref> during the [[Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]], after having been abandoned, the former [[Sith apprentice]] [[Asajj Ventress]] rejoined her long-lost family of the Nightsisters<ref name="Nightsisters">{{TCW|Nightsisters (episode)}}</ref> and, aided by them, attempted to assassinate her [[Sith Master|master]], the Separatist [[Head of State]] [[Count]] [[Dooku]],<ref name="Witches of the Mist">{{TCW|Witches of the Mist}}</ref> and the [[Kaleesh]] [[warlord]] [[General]] [[Grievous]] and the [[Separatist Droid Army]] were dispatched by Dooku to completely [[Battle of Dathomir|wipe out]] the Nightsisters in retaliation for the machinations of Nightsister [[Mother (title)|Mother]] [[Talzin]] against him.<ref name="Massacre">{{TCW|Massacre}}</ref>

After the battle<ref name="Massacre" /> only several Nightsisters remained,<ref name="Fallen Order" /><ref name="GoD">''[[Ghosts of Dathomir]]''</ref> including Deathstick.<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" /> At that time, her mother had left her homeworld long before her people were [[Death|slaughtered]].<ref name="SWU" /> As a [[Youngling|child]], Deathstick carried the weight of knowing that she was once part of something larger than herself that was taken away from her.<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" />

Growing up in the [[Anoat sector]] of [[the galaxy]]'s Outer Rim Territories, Deathstick began her criminal career as a hired [[assassin]]. Through a series of spectacularly public murders of gang leaders, she ended up moving into the drug trade. At some point, she had a run-in with the [[Galactic Empire]] that cost her a great deal of her face,<ref name="SWU People" /> causing her to have an entire prosthetic lower face.<ref name="Crimson Reign 1" /> This prompted her to wear a mask that only left her [[eye]]s visible.<ref name="SWU People">{{WebCite|url=https://playstarwarsuprising.com/game-guide/lore/#people|text=''Star Wars: Uprising'' Lore: People|archivedate=20160619115943}}</ref>

===Imperial Era===
====Leading the Kouhun====
By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War]], Deathstick was the leader of the [[Kouhun (order)|Kouhun]], a highly secretive and structured order of assassins based on [[Mataou]].<ref name="SWU" /> Under her leadership, the Kouhun murdered several high profile individuals in the sector, including the [[Ubrik Adelhard's wife|wife]] of [[Governor]] [[Ubrik Adelhard]].<ref name="OS Locations">{{WebCite|url=https://playstarwarsuprising.com/game-guide/introduction/#locations|text=Locations|archivedate=20160624013822}}</ref>

====Working for Crimson Dawn====
{{Quote|My name is Deathstick, and I bear a message. Crimson Dawn is inviting the leaders of all other syndicates to a…gathering.|Deathstick|<ref name="BH15">[[Bounty Hunters 15|''Bounty Hunters'' 15]]</ref>}}
In [[3 ABY]],<ref name="WOFTBH date">''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' dates the [[War of the Bounty Hunters]] crossover to [[3 ABY]].</ref> Deathstick was hired by [[Lady]] [[Qi'ra]] of [[Crimson Dawn]] to join her as an assassin. She told Deathstick that [[Emperor]] [[Darth Sidious|Palpatine]] was behind the death of her people, and that by joining her she could see the Empire fall.<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" /> She was tasked to hunt down the [[bounty hunter]]s [[Beilert Valance]] and [[Dengar]] on [[Nar Shaddaa]]. While Valance conversed with arms dealer, [[Devono Vix]] on his knowledge about Crimson Dawn, Deathstick shot Vix's head off, killing him. She then shot Valance in the [[Limb|arm]], wounding him and damaging his core.<ref name="BH13" />

[[File:DeathstickDominates-BountyHunters14.png|thumb|right|250px|Deathstick and her droids lay waste to Mama Stammoch's crew.]]
Valance and Dengar managed to escape in a [[Starship|ship]]. They then thought they saw Deathstick aboard the ship, but they played it off as being nothing. However, Deathstick quickly tracked them to the hideout of [[Mama Stammoch]] and her crew. Upon reaching the hideout, she overheard Stammoch warn Valance and Dengar that the assassin would kill them all. When Dengar suggested that she was overreacting, Deathstick blew up the entrance to the hideout and assured Dengar that she wasn't. Upon entering the hideout, Deathstick and her droids laid waste to most of Stammoch's crew, forcing her, Valance, and Dengar to make an escape. Throwing a [[thermal detonator]], they managed to blow a hole through the wall and flee from the hideout in a [[landspeeder]]. The three of them were chased by Deathstick's droids, but they jumped out of the speeder as it crashed, getting the droids off their trail. However, Deathstick had still not lost her targets and kept an eye on them as she contacted Qi'ra. When she insisted on killing them, Qi'ra told her that herding them in the right direction was an important part of her plan. She then assured the assassin that she would get to kill them soon enough.<ref name="BH14">[[Bounty Hunters 14|''Bounty Hunters'' 14]]</ref>

[[File:DeathstickWine-BountyHunters15.png|thumb|left|150px|Deathstick meets General Vukorah.]]
Deathstick then traveled to [[Coronet City]] on the [[planet]] [[Corellia]] where she entered the [[Unbroken Clan safe house]]. She helped herself to the [[spice wine]] while waiting for [[Unbroken Clan]] [[General]] [[Vukorah]] to enter the room she was in. When Vukorah and her [[Mimbanese]] underling entered the room, Vukorah shot the Mimbanese with suspicion that he either sold her out or was incompetent. Deathstick gave Vukorah an invitation from the Crimson Dawn to the [[Auction for Han Solo]],<ref name="BH15" /> an auction for various crime syndicates to bid for the [[Carbonite|carbonated]] body of [[smuggler]] [[Han Solo]].<ref name="WOTBH 2">[[War of the Bounty Hunters 2|''War of the Bounty Hunters'' 2]]</ref> She then travelled to the [[city]] of [[Canto Bight]]<ref name="BH15" /> on the planet [[Cantonica]].<ref name="CB Databank">{{Databank|canto-bight|Canto Bight}}</ref> She ambushed Dengar and Valance who were looking for someone to tell them the location of the Auction for Han Solo. She kicked Dengar and not allowing Valance to get a clear shot. Deathstick threw a [[thermal detonator]]. After kicking Dengar into a crate and Valance of the edge of the platform, Deathstick took an emergency contact device and left. However, Dengar swiped a device off of her with the location of the auction.<ref name="BH15" />

Heading to the<ref name="BH16">[[Bounty Hunters 16|''Bounty Hunters'' 16]]</ref> [[Water|ice]] planet<ref name="SW14">[[Star Wars (2020) 14|''Star Wars'' (2020) 14]]</ref> of [[Jekara]] where the Crimson Dawn starship, the [[Vermillion (Crimson Dawn)|''Vermillion'']] was landed, Deathstick saved Dengar from a [[zugorak]] that was about to attack him. In exchange for saving him, Dengar gave Deathstick a drive with the location of [[Cadeliah]] on it.<ref name="BH16" />

[[File:DeathstickSneak-2020DoctorAphra13.png|thumb|right|270px|Deathstick sneaking up from the shadow of ceiling.]]
Deathstick then boarded the ''Vermillion'', where she hid and watched as [[Gallin Crae]], [[Just Lucky]], and [[Ariole Yu]] engaged in combat. While Lucky pointed a [[sniper rifle]] at Crae, Deathstick suddenly threw multiple bladed projectiles containing [[Dathomirian poison]] from the shadow of the ceiling at Lucky, one slicing off part of his blaster and others inflicting cuts all over him. Deathstick leaped down from the ceiling, pointing her [[blaster rifle]] at Lucky and Yu, and suggested to Crae to keep only one of the two alive. Gallin refused her request and she proceeded to shoot Lucky and Yu with a stunning harpoon to disable both of them. She then told them how Lucky will be dead in ten minutes to the poison inflicted from her throwing blades if they do not surrender.<ref name="DA13">[[Doctor Aphra (2020) 13|''Doctor Aphra'' (2020) 13]]</ref> She then had the two locked up in a cell, however they escaped with help from [[Chelli Lona Aphra|Doctor Aphra]] and [[Sana Starros]]. When the four entered the art gallery of the ship, they found Qi'ra and [[Margo]] talking, unaware of the intruders. When Qi'ra left however, Deathstick appeared at the scene again leaping down from the ceiling readying for an attack, while Lucky shot at her to no avail.<ref name="DA14">[[Doctor Aphra (2020) 14|''Doctor Aphra'' (2020) 14]]</ref> 

[[File:AphraKnifepoint-2020DoctorAphra15.png|thumb|left|250px|Deathstick catches Doctor Aphra aboard Vermillion.]]
Lucky, Yu, Starros, and Aphra hid behind display cases in the gallery, as Deathstick went on the offensive and shot viciously at them with her blaster. Deathstick then charged at them with a massive leap, throwing bladed projectiles in Aphra and Lucky's direction, missing them barely but causing them to stumble out of their cover. Aphra fled as Deathstick leaped to chase her and threw more blades at her, successfully pinning Aphra to the wall by her clothes. Deathstick then put her [[knife]] coated with the Dathomirian [[poison]] to Aphra's throat, cautioning her from moving and cutting herself to the poisoned blades. However, Yu and Starros rallied behind Deathstick and began shooting at her, causing her to swiftly disengage and disappear. Deathstick then alerted Crimson Dawn guards to reinforce the situation at the gallery, re-engaging in combat. Amidst the shootout, Aphra noticed a [[Thought Dowser|thought dowser]] in the gallery and used her [[electro-tattoo]]s to activate it. The mind-controlling device caused Deathstick and the Crimson Dawn guards to freeze and fetch Deathstick's poisoned blades from her belt-pouch and to stab themselves with them against their own will, giving time for the four to escape while taunting Deathstick as she was enduring the stab wound and poison on the ground. However, Aphra and Starros abandoned Lucky and Yu in the hangar during their escape.<ref name="DA15">[[Doctor Aphra (2020) 15|''Doctor Aphra'' (2020) 15]]</ref> Shortly after, wounded Deathstick and Gallin Crae stumbled after them into the hangar. Deathstick surviving her own deadly poisons, still questioned why she still could not kill them, when Qi'ra walked in and refused Deathstick's requests and instead invited Lucky and Yu for a chat.<ref name="DA16">[[Doctor Aphra (2020) 16|''Doctor Aphra'' (2020) 16]]</ref> Deathstick was later present in the room when Qi'ra chatted with Lucky and Yu, observing silently from the background while sipping [[Alderaan]] [[Starlight brandy]].<ref name="DA21">[[Doctor Aphra (2020) 21|''Doctor Aphra'' (2020) 21]]</ref>

[[File:DeathstickUnmasked-CrimsonReign-1.png|thumb|right|150px|Deathstick meditates in her private quarters without her mask on.]]
Sometime after the events involving Doctor Aphra and her company in the gallery, Qi'ra had gathered Deathstick and other prominent Crimson Dawn members together and briefed them on the upcoming "Reign."<ref name="WoTBH 5">[[War of the Bounty Hunters 5|''War of the Bounty Hunters'' 5]]</ref> Deathstick withdrew to her residence, meditating privately and readying herself for the next mission anticipated by Lady Qi'ra.<ref name="Crimson Reign 1" /> Deathstick contacted a member of Crimson Dawn and continued their plot to capture Cadeliah. The transmission was intercepted by a [[T'onga's crew|group]] of bounty hunters. Deathstick was later notified about the interception of transmissions and told the Crimson Dawn member to assume the bounty hunters know everything.<ref name="BH20">[[Bounty Hunters 20|''Bounty Hunters'' 20]]</ref> Later on, Deathstick went to meet Qi'ra for a mission assignment aboard the ''Vermillion'', where she met [[Ochi]] briefly on her way to the meeting. The two showed mistrust against each other during the tense banter and Deathstick noted to herself afterwards that Ochi was a crazy bastard.<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" />

She then entered the offices to meet Qi'ra, Margo, and [[Trinia]] who briefed her about the current [[Syndicate War|war]] between the criminal syndicates in the galaxy. Qi'ra gave Deathstick a [[datacard]] that had information about her next target, Cadeliah, who would be taking refuge and hiding with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]] from the Imperials. The target surprised Deathstick and prompted Qi'ra to ask Deathstick if there was some kind of a problem with it, in which she answered that there is none as long as she gets her credits.<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" />

[[File:DeathstickCamps-CrimsonReign2.png|thumb|left|150px|Deathstick examines the hologram of Cadeliah by a campfire.]]
Deathstick left the ''Vermillion'' and camped alone in the woods on her way to Cadeliah, examining her hologram from the datacard in the gloom of the evening. Sometime later Deathstick managed to find the location of the rebel base within [[Panisia]] in the Outer Rim Territories. She scouted the base from a distance with [[binoculars]], searching for any signs of Cadeliah, finally finding her sitting idle in the open. Noting the large amount of rebels bustling within the base, Deathstick resorted to contact anonymously the [[Imperial Anti-Terrorism Watchline]] and reveal the location of the rebel base to the Imperials to cause a distraction and opportunity to reach Cadeliah in the ensuing chaos. She would then successfully capture Cadeliah, who tried to defend herself with a knife to no avail.<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" />

[[File:CadeliahCaptured-CrimsonReign2.png|thumb|right|200px|Deathstick delivers Cadeliah to Crimson Dawn.]]
Deathstick returned to the ''Vermillion'' with a captured Cadeliah and handed her over to Qi'ra. Deathstick curiously inquired from her about what she would do with Cadeliah, in which Qi'ra answered that her job was to only deliver the girl to her and now on Cadeliah's fate would belong to Qi'ra.<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" />

When Qi'ra and [[the Archivist]] were preparing to use a device called [[Fermata Cage]] once again against [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] and [[Darth Sidious]] despite losing several valuable Crimson Dawn allies in the events surrounding the previous attempt, Qi'ra asked this time Deathstick to defend the Archivist while the device was operated. Deathstick wished Qi'ra well and told her that she was no warrior nor a guard, but an assassin, and that she had already done everything she had asked of her. Deathstick then refused to aid Qi'ra further with her plan, telling her to find someone else for the job.<ref name="Hidden Empire 4">[[Hidden Empire 4|''Hidden Empire'' 4]]</ref>

====Part of T'onga's crew====
[[File:DurgeAndDeathstick-BountyHunters35.png|thumb|left|250px|Deathstick and Durge fighting together on D'assem]]
After ending the partnership with the Crimson Dawn Deathstick spent time in the [[Bounty Hunters Guild Social Club]] at a black-market district on the planet [[D'Assem|D'assem]]. When T'onga arrived at the club to meet [[Khel Tanna]] to negotiate about hiring a new crew for her mission, a bar-brawl ensued where Deathstick allied with [[Durge]] to beat everyone up. After Khel Tanna saw the two be the last ones standing, she was impressed and hired Deathstick and Durge to T'onga's crew.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 35" />

Now part of T'onga's crew, Deathstick travelled along side the others to go after [[Boba Fett]]. On the ''[[Enigma Catalyst]]'' she watched the other crew members bicker with each other and thought this new crew should be fun. Deathstick broke inside T'onga's personal quarters in the ship when T'onga was resting to briefly chat with her, and warned her that Boba Fett would be unusually dangerous this time around as he had a big bounty on his head put on by the Black Sun Syndicate.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 35" />

Later T'onga's crew successfully tracked and found the hiding Boba Fett on the [[Rishi Moon]], which ensued into a fight against him. After Boba Fett had taken out several crew members during their fight, Deathstick saw her chances and disarmed Boba Fett of his knife with a throwing blade as he was busy brawling with [[Bossk'wassak'Cradossk]], but was swiftly after caught in a [[stun net]] when Boba retaliated against her. Eventually, T'onga revealed that they were actually there to hire Fett for a job, which he accepted. However, Fett claimed that the group would have to do a job for him first.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 35" />

[[File:TongasCrewInSlaveI-BountyHunters36.png|thumb|right|250px|Deathstick and T'onga's crew on Slave I.]]
After forming up a partnership with Boba Fett, the crew and Deathstick travelled on [[Slave I]] to the moon [[Quantxi]] to assist Fett deal with a [[Black Sun]] gangster [[Crime lord|Lord]] [[Xanak Grunseit]], who had put the bounty on his head. The crew pretended to have captured Boba Fett and delivered him to the Black Sun leader. While the crew escorted Fett into the base of the gangsters to claim their reward on the capture of Boba Fett, the Black Sun betrayed them and held the crew at gun point, intending to eliminate them all instead of paying up. Boba Fett would then be the first one to act and initiate violent confrontation with the Black Sun, managing to instantly assassinate Xanak by throwing [[Syntox]] at his face. In the ensuing battle Deathstick vaulted around, felling gangsters with her throwing blades and blurted that she knew this crew would be fun indeed. After surviving the mission on Quantxi and having helped Fett with his bounty, Fett rewarded T'onga with coordinates to [[Tarr Kligson]], a cyborg fixer, and claimed that this man would be able to help save Beilert Valance, which had now became the main goal for T'onga's crew.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 36">[[Bounty Hunters 36|''Bounty Hunters'' 36]]</ref>

[[File:BosskBickers-BountyHunters37.png|thumb|left|250px|T'onga's crew waiting for news about Valance.]]The bounty hunter crew went on to travel on ''Enigma Catalyst'' to the coordinates that Boba Fett had given them and found a large space station floating in the remote location within Outer Rim Territories. They entered the docking bay of the space station and were immediately welcomed in by [[Telemak]], the guardian of the station, and a group of other accompanying droids. The droids then took Beilert Valance further inside the station for treatment, but requested that the bounty hunters should wait in the docking bay in the meantime. The crew started to get bored waiting as the hours went by and there were no news of Valance, which prompted the crew to start investigating despite Telemak's request to stay put. T'onga told Zuckuss and 4-LOM to break off from the crew to get intel from the droids deeper in the station, while Deathstick and the rest of the crew would still stay waiting in the docking bay for the time being.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 37">[[Bounty Hunters 37|''Bounty Hunters'' 37]]</ref>

While 4-LOM and Zuckuss continued their secret investigations and were soon caught for it by the droids of the station, the station's guardian Telemak appeared back in the docking bay to inform the crew that they were of course welcome to visit the ''haven'', but that it would have been preferred for them to wait in the docking bay. T'onga then chastised Telemak for letting the hours go by without news about the condition of Valance, demanding update on the situation, which prompted Deathstick to ask Durge if he too got the feeling that they will be blasting their way out soon before this all is done. Unbeknownst to the crew, Tarr Kligson had controlled Telemak all their time on the station and perceived everything through the droid. He then asked the bounty hunters to wait for a little bit longer and assured them that T'onga would see Valance soon again.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 38">[[Bounty Hunters 38|''Bounty Hunters'' 38]]</ref>

After more waiting, Telemak shut down in the docking bay, causing the bounty hunters to investigate and bicker with each other. Soon Deathstick would inform the crew that something is happening, as the door to the docking bay opened and revealed Beilert Valance accompanied by Tarr Cligson himself. Once again, unbeknownst to the crew, Cligson had secretly reprogrammed Valance<ref name="Bounty Hunters 38" /> into a vessel for the Scourge,<ref name="Bounty Hunters 39">[[Bounty Hunters 39|''Bounty Hunters'' 39]]</ref> who then suddenly turned against his friends and went on to blast [[Catak]] with his palm blaster as he tried to calm Valance down and converse with him disguised as [[Yuralla Vega]].<ref name="Bounty Hunters 38" /> The Scourge-infected Valance quickly departed the station, leaving Cligson to the mercy of the bounty hunters.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 39" /> 4-LOM was also separated from Zuckuss, escaping into the galaxy at Zuckuss's urging to flee the Scourge.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 38" />

====Part of Tanna's crew====
Deathstick secretly agreed to overthrow T'onga and install Tanna as the [[Khel Tanna's crew|crew's]] new leader. Just as the crew reached Valance's current location on the planet [[Epikonia]], Deathstick, Durge, Bossk, and Tanna turned on T'onga and Zuckuss, who were both thrown from the '' Enigma Catalyst'' and onto the Scourge-infested world.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 40">[[Bounty Hunters 40|''Bounty Hunters'' 40]]</ref> As Deathstick and her compatriots took up a new life under Tanna's leadership,<ref name="Bounty Hunters 41">[[Bounty Hunters 41|''Bounty Hunters'' 41]]</ref> T'onga and Zuckuss, aided by T'onga's wife [[Losha Tarkon]] and former foe [[Vukorah]], were actually able to restore Valance to his senses and back up his memories up to the [[Attack on the Executor|Attack on the ''Executor'']] thanks to data provided by defected [[Imperial officer]] [[Jyala Haydenn]].<ref name="Bounty Hunters 40" />

Deathstick and her allies embarked on a successful bounty hunting career. One week after the [[Death Match on Tatooine]] carried out by Valance and his allies, who had reunited with 4-LOM by that time, Deathstick and the rest of Tanna's crew were celebrated one of their successful bounties in a [[cantina]], where Deathstick noticed Durge had finished his drink. As Deathstick, Durge, and Tanna discussed that, however, Bossk remained by himself, pondering if he had made a mistake in betraying T'onga and Zuckuss.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 40" /> At some point, Deathstick and her allies returned to the Bounty Hunters Guild Social Club on D'Assem, where Valance and many of his allies also happened to be.<ref name="Tall Tales">{{StoryCite|story=Tall Tales|book=Revelations (2023) 1}}</ref>

====Iron Blockade====
[[File:Uprising_LoadingScreen_Tip_Deathstick.png|thumb|right|250px|Deathstick and her Kouhun.]]
Several [[Standard month|months]] into the [[Iron Blockade]] between [[4 ABY]] and [[5 ABY]], Deathstick was introduced at the [[Shadow Market]] on [[Bespin]], to [[the Smuggler]], a young scoundrel from [[Burnin Konn]] who had forged an alliance with the [[Ivax Syndicate]] and the [[Noble Court]]-in-exile. Shortly thereafter, the Smuggler underwent and passed the initiation rites of the Kouhun. Although Deathstick did not think the scoundrel could embrace the Kouhun's philosophy, she nonetheless agreed to join the anti-Empire alliance, which came to be known as the [[Uprising]]. As their first major joint operation, the Nobles, the Syndicate and the Kouhun made plans to abduct [[Commander]] [[Bragh]], leader of the deadly Imperial [[Purge Trooper (Anoat sector)|Purge Troopers]]. It was Deathstick who outlined the plan and provided some of her assassins.<ref name="SWU" />

However, the planned ambush went awry as the Smuggler's sister [[Riley]] and the Ivax Syndicate sold out the Uprising and exposed their plan to the Imperials in hopes of having their [[death mark]]s removed. In the ensuing fight on [[Anoat]] between the Uprising and the Imperials, many members of the Kouhun and other allies were captured, while Deathstick managed to escape and go into hiding. Soon after the incident she contacted the Smuggler through a [[Comlink|communicator]]. As the Smuggler began to explain the unfortunate turn of events, she told him that if she wanted him dead, he would already be painfully and possibly hilariously dead. Deathstick then asked him if it was the Ivax Syndicate who betrayed them, in which the Smuggler responded that it did not matter and told Deathstick to leave Riley alone. Getting a sufficient enough confirmation out of the Smuggler, Deathstick ended the call abruptly with a goodbye.<ref name="SWU" />

Deathstick would later appear suddenly in a communicator call between the Smuggler and [[Loathe]] when they both were in the middle of rescuing the members of the Uprising from [[The Crypt (prison)|the Crypt]]. Taking part in the rescue operation herself, she assigned Loathe to delay the Imperial forces as the prisoners made their escape, stating that no Kouhun should live to be captured and that Loathe's sacrifice would redeem the rest. Deathstick would then admit she was too quick to dismiss the Smuggler earlier, expressing her gratitude for saving the members of the Kouhun from the Crypt. Once the Smuggler objected about sacrificing Loathe, she told him that he was not of Kouhun and it was not his business to involve in their internal matters.<ref name="SWU" />

Deathstick then contacted Riley and told her that her Kouhun were causing chaos within the Crypt and transmitted the location Loathe had acquired of [[Her Majesty]]'s prison cell to Riley and the Smuggler for their rescue mission.<ref name="SWU" />

[[File:DeathstickTrailer-Uprising.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Deathstick and her allies of the Uprising]]
Soon after the Uprising were successful in the rescue mission and partially successful in their original objective in taking out Commander Bragh, Deathstick was present during the meeting in [[Paradise Atrium]] between the members of the Uprising, including the Rebel Alliance led by [[Tam Bastion]], to discuss about the future of their allied resistance movement and the looming threat of [[Ubrik Adelhard's Imperial remnant|Adelhard's Imperial remnant]]. While the accusations flew around and the tensions between the groups ran high, she would joke during the meeting about giving into her temptations and try killing all the others, especially [[Voras]], whose Ivax Syndicate was accused of betrayal by many others of the various groups present. The Smuggler managed to reason with the groups and defuse the situation, and when he demanded acknowledgment from everyone for why their Uprising had been successful in the first place, Deathstick gave a hint that their mutual co-operation within the Uprising was a key to success, prompting Smuggler to successfully persuade others to the cause. Alongside all the other groups present, Deathstick would then accept the invitation from the Smuggler to keep continuing their Uprising with him acting as the leader, stating only mysteriously that she and her Kouhun had their reasons to be in with the alliance.<ref name="SWU" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|You're not fighting for family or money. With the Kouhun, you act because you can.|Deathstick|<ref name="SWU" />}}
[[File:Assassin_Deathstick_Render.png|thumb|right|150px|Deathstick]]
Deathstick was interested in powerful and capable people who had the knack for survival, though she occasionally liked toying with and taunt such individuals in humorous fashion. Sometimes she offered such individuals an opportunity to challenge themselves to go through the initiation ritual of Kouhun, observing their capabilities and potential.<ref name="SWU" />

Deathstick killed, because after the massacre of her people, she felt alone and wanted other people to feel that way as well. She also claimed to Qi'ra that she wasn't a chatty type of person<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" /> and many times had a habit of having her arms crossed across her chest when she stood idle or conversing with other people.<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" /><ref name="Hidden Empire 4">[[Hidden Empire 4|''Hidden Empire'' 4]]</ref><ref name="Bounty Hunters 35">[[Bounty Hunters 35|''Bounty Hunters'' 35]]</ref><ref name="Bounty Hunters 36">[[Bounty Hunters 36|''Bounty Hunters'' 36]]</ref><ref name="Bounty Hunters 38">[[Bounty Hunters 38|''Bounty Hunters'' 38]]</ref>

Deathstick was impatient at times. When she was hired by Crimson Dawn, Deathstick asked Qi'ra why she could not just kill Beilert Valance and Dengar when she had a clear shot, questioning the plan.<ref name="BH14" /> Deathstick did not like when operations or plans took too long, and would take control in order to speed it up.<ref name="DA13" /> Deathstick was also very self-assured and amoral, and occasionally had a clipped manner of speech.<ref name="SWU People" />

Deathstick loved physical conflicts and believed in acting because it was possible, not for family or money.<ref name="SWU" /><ref name="SWU People" /> She also loved collecting exotics.<ref name="SWU People" />

==Skills and abilities==
{{Dialogue|Mama Stammoch|You were attacked by ''Deathstick''…and you came ''here?!'' You've just killed us all!|Dengar|Don't you think you're overreacting?|Deathstick|I assure you that she is ''not'' overreacting.|attr=Mama Stammoch, Dengar and Deathstick|src=<ref name="BH14" />}}
[[File:DeathstickDengarValance-BountyHunters15.png|thumb|left|250px|Deathstick fights against Dengar and Valance.]]
Deathstick was talented in combat, knowing how to use exotic [[weapon]]ry.<ref name="DA15" /> She was also adept in unarmed [[Unarmed combat|hand-to-hand combat]], being able to take on multiple opponents at the same time,<ref name="BH15" /> and had extraordinary agility that made her able to evade [[blaster]] fire directed at her<ref name="BH14" /> and do daring leaps and vaults.<ref name="DA13" /><ref name="DA15" /> She favored the usage of shadows and guile when confronting her opponents,<ref name="SWU" /> and her skills also extended to the usage of various gadgets, like [[droid]]s and explosives.<ref name="BH14" />

[[File:Deathstick_vs_Stormtrooper_Uprising_Launch_Trailer.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Deathstick takes out a stormtrooper with her vibroblades during the Iron Blockade.]]
Deathstick was also a skilled infiltrator and tracker, capable of breaking into safe-houses and following her targets throughout the galaxy.<ref name="BH15" /> She was also quite observant, noting changes in respiration rate of others and deducing information based on that;<ref name="Bounty Hunters 35">[[Bounty Hunters 35|''Bounty Hunters'' 35]]</ref> and being usually the first one to notice if there was a sudden change coming up in situations.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 36">[[Bounty Hunters 36|''Bounty Hunters'' 36]]</ref><ref name="Bounty Hunters 38">[[Bounty Hunters 38|''Bounty Hunters'' 38]]</ref> She also had skills in leadership roles and in strategy, devising assassination plans and directing the members of the Kouhun order.<ref name="SWU" />

Despite being a daughter of a [[Force-sensitive]] Dathomirian Nightsister, it was unclear whether or not Deathstick herself possessed any [[Force power]]s, although when the young Smuggler from Burnin Konn wished to pursue and learn [[Dark side of the Force|dark side]] powers from Shelish, he found out that Deathstick had risen to her current mysterious power stature through her mother during the events of Iron Blockade.<ref name="SWU" /> She was also seemingly able to just appear and vanish in places on a whim.<ref name="Bounty Hunters 35">[[Bounty Hunters 35|''Bounty Hunters'' 35]]</ref><ref name="DA15">[[Doctor Aphra (2020) 15|''Doctor Aphra'' (2020) 15]]</ref>

==Equipment==
{{Quote|I wouldn't move. Those knives are coated in an old Dathomirian poison. It only takes ten minutes to—|Deathstick|<ref name="BH15" />}}
[[File:Deathstick_and_allies_Uprising_Announce_Trailer.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Deathstick and her allies of the Uprising preparing weapons.]]
Deathstick had an arsenal of weaponry like various bladed melee and throwing weapons,<ref name="DA15" /> and she had a unique [[scope]]d blaster that could shoot debilitating harpoons capable of stunning<ref name="DA13" /> or outright killing the target.<ref name="BH14" /> She also utilized a [[staff]] for close combat<ref name="Bounty Hunters 35" /> and sometimes coated her blades in Dathomirian poison.<ref name="DA15" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The mobile game, I got to play one of the coolest characters who has ever existed in the world. Her name's Deathstick, she's the coolest and she's strong....She's just the best at what she does and she's got a real deep voice.|Sara Cravens, voice actor for Deathstick|<ref>{{YouTube|video=B9g_VUxYd2w|text=The Codec Sara Cravens| Starwars | Batman | Performance Matters|channel=NationFusion|name=NationFusion|date=2016-10-24}}</ref>}}
[[File:Deathstick_concept_art.jpg|thumb|Concept art by Brian Matyas]]
Deathstick first appeared the [[2015]] [[Star Wars video games|mobile game]], ''[[Star Wars: Uprising]]'', developed by [[Kabam]]. She was voiced by actress [[Sara Cravens]].<ref name="SWU" /> In early concept art by [[Brian Matyas]], Deathstick had green eyes and the right side of her face was prosthetic as well.<ref name="DeathstickConceptBM">{{ArtStation|subdomain=bmatyas|url=projects/n6yA6|name=Brian Matyas|text=Deathstick}}</ref> [[Ethan Sacks]], writer of ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunters]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic books series]], decided to bring Deathstick back because he felt that she had a backstory fitting nicely into what was happening with Crimson Dawn.<ref name="ESTwitter">{{Twitter|ethanjsacks|status/1500446847785127943|[[Ethan Sacks]]|quote=She had such a fantastic presence, and a backstory that fit nicely into what was happening with Crimson Dawn.|archivedate=20220306122332}}</ref>

''Uprising'' portrays Deathstick as a [[Dathomirian]] [[Zabrak]].<ref name="SWU" /> Multiple comics set earlier in the timeline, however, depict Deathstick as a [[human]].<ref name="BH15" /><ref name="DA15" /><ref name="Crimson Reign 1" /><ref name="Crimson Reign 2" /> ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy]]'' later affirmed that Deathstick was Dathomirian.<ref name="SWE" /> This article assumes the original depiction is accurate.

In [[Crimson Reign 2|''Crimson Reign'' 2]], Qi'ra states that Deathstick and Shelish escaped the Battle of Dathomir.<ref name="Crimson Reign 2" /> However, this contradicts ''Uprising'' and ''[[Dawn of Rebellion]]'', which both state that Shelish left years before the event even happened.<ref name="SWU" /><ref name="DoR">''[[Dawn of Rebellion]]''</ref> This article assumes that the latter mentioned sources are accurate.
